title: Colymbada toletana
intro: Colymbada toletana, also known as Tolero or Tolero's Colymbada, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Colymbada toletana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, which are used to treat conditions like gastritis, stomach ulcers, and menstrual disorders.
taxonomy: Colymbada toletana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Colymbada and is native to the Mediterranean region. Colymbada toletana is a type of dandelion and is closely related to the genus Taraxacum, which includes the common dandelion. The plant is typically found in dry, rocky areas and is known for its bright yellow flowers. Colymbada toletana is often used as a food source for various insects and small animals in its native habitat.
